carbon_policy_signal.item: EU-UK ETS linkage deal postponed following Keir Starmer resignation; July 22 summit reschedule date TBD.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: Delay defers UKA-EUA spread arbitrage clarity and cross-border hedging mechanics; valuation uncertainty on both blocs persists until summit rescheduled.

freshness_caveat: Coal data as of 2025-12-26 (181 days old) and Clean Dark as of 2025-12-31 (176 days old) are stale; current analysis relies on fresh DE Power, GB Power, TTF, storage, and EUA (all ≤1 day). Coal percentile and Clean Dark spreads are outdated and should not inform intraday trading.
risk_flags: Storage at 47.2% (22nd percentile, 14.5 pp below seasonal)—refill pace critical to H2 power curve and winter gas security.
Asian LNG bidding outcompeting EU importers; structural margin compression for gas-fired DA dispatch if winter fill undershoots.
Heatwave reducing hydro inflows and thermal plant availability simultaneously; DA power volatility elevated through week.
EU-UK ETS linkage postponed—timing of UKA-EUA spread normalization and cross-border hedging now uncertain; policy risk on methane rules persists.

base.summary: Heatwave persists, hydro/thermal constrained, Clean Spark elevated, storage build slow, gas soft on weak demand.

downside.summary: Heat breaks mid-week, hydro inflows recover, thermal units return online, DA supply pressure eases, gas seasonal softness dominates.

upside.summary: Heatwave extends, cooling-water scarcity worsens thermal derate, hydro collapse, Asian LNG bids spike—gas and power front-month rally.

themes: Extreme thermal dispatch economics—Clean Spark 106 EUR/MWh
EU gas tightness from Asian LNG competition and below-seasonal storage
Heatwave-driven hydro constraint and cooling-water scarcity
ETS linkage postponement defers cross-bloc arbitrage clarity
top_drivers: Extreme heat driving 226% monthly surge in DE Power (200.1 EUR/MWh, 87th percentile) and simultaneous hydro-thermal derate.
Clean Spark at 106 EUR/MWh (99th percentile, +154 EUR/MWh monthly) signals gas-to-coal switching collapsed; coal at 7th percentile underscores thermal scarcity.
Storage 14.5 pp below seasonal, Asian LNG bidding competition, and geopolitical uncertainty—structural gas tightness for H2 refill.
top_takeaway: Clean Spark at 99th-percentile amid extreme heat, storage 14.5 pp below seasonal, and Asian LNG competition threaten winter refill—gas structural tightness dominates.

watchlist.metric: EU storage refill pace vs. seasonal baseline
watchlist.why: Storage 14.5 pp below normal; Asian LNG competition may further constrain July–August build, extending Cal+1 and Cal+2 gas premium.

watchlist.metric: Heatwave hydro derate and thermal cooling-water constraints
watchlist.why: High temps boost AC load and reduce hydro generation; simultaneous thermal unit derate from cooling-water scarcity drives immediate DA power volatility and upside tail risk.

watchlist.metric: LNG auction results and Asian buyer participation
watchlist.why: Direct TTF arbitrage signal; if EU loses cargoes to China, spot gas tightness accelerates, reinforcing Clean Spark premium and forward curve strength.

watchlist.metric: EU-UK ETS linkage summit reschedule date
watchlist.why: Postponement from July 22 defers UKA-EUA spread mechanics clarity; cross-border hedging and sectoral profitability outlook remain suspended.

watchlist.metric: Methane reduction rule Commission softening signal
watchlist.why: Upstream capex efficiency and future EU gas import terms at stake; regulatory relief or stall will influence medium-term TTF risk premium.

watchlist_dated.name: Heatwave hydro impact (intraday)
watchlist_dated.why: High temps reduce water availability for thermal cooling and hydro generation; DA power spreads widen, Cal+1 rally risk.
